com.mentor.chs.plugin.styles

Interface IXGraphicsAttribute


  • @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXGraphicsAttribute
    This specifies graphics attribute values to be applied on an object. The following can be specified
    attribute default value
    line style IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.NO_STYLE
    line thickness 0
    primary color null
    secondary color null
    fill pattern FILL_PATTERN_STYLE.PATTERN_INHERIT
    fill foreground color null
    fill background color null

    The secondary color is applied only if the object is IXWire and line style is overidden that is different from IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.NO_STYLE and IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.SOLID.
    The fill foreground and fill background color are applied only if fill pattern is not FILL_PATTERN_STYLE.PATTERN_NONE

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Interface and Description
      static class  IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Deprecated Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.awt.Color getFillBackgroundColor()
      Gets the fill background color.
      java.awt.Color getFillForegroundColor()
      Gets the fill foreground color.
      FILL_PATTERN_STYLE getFillPattern()
      Gets the fill pattern.
      IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE getLineStyle()
      Gets the specified line style.
      int getLineThickness()
      Deprecated. 
      You will not ba able to get physical thicknesses with this method. Please use getThickness() method instead.
      java.awt.Color getPrimaryColor()
      Gets the specified primary color.
      java.awt.Color getSecondaryColor()
      Gets the specified secondary color.
      IXGraphicSize getThickness()
      Gets the specified line thickness.
      void setFillBackgroundColor(java.awt.Color fillBackgroundColor)
      Sets the background color to the specified value.
      void setFillForegroundColor(java.awt.Color fillForegroundColor)
      Sets the foreground color to the specified value.
      void setFillPattern(FILL_PATTERN_STYLE fillPattern)
      Sets the fill pattern to the specified value.
      If the pattern set to FILL_PATTERN_STYLE.PATTERN_NONE, the fill foreground color and fill background colors are ignored.
      void setLineStyle(IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE lineStyle)
      Sets the line style.
      void setLineThickness(int thickness)
      Deprecated. 
      You will not be able to set physical thicknesses with this method. Please use setThickness(IXGraphicSize) method or setThickness(double, XGraphicsUnit) method instead.
      void setPrimaryColor(java.awt.Color primaryColor)
      Sets the primary color to the specified value.
      void setSecondaryColor(java.awt.Color secondaryColor)
      Sets the secondary color to the specified value.
      void setThickness(double value, XGraphicsUnit unit)
      Sets the line thickness to the specified value.
      void setThickness(IXGraphicSize thickness)
      Sets the line thickness to the specified value.

    • Method Detail

      • setLineStyle

        void setLineStyle(IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE lineStyle)
        Sets the line style.
        Parameters:
        lineStyle - one of the following values
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.NO_STYLE
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.SOLID
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.DASHED
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.DOTTED
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.LONG_DASH
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.LONG_DASH_DOT
        • IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.LONG_DASH_DOT_DOT

      • setLineThickness

        @Deprecated
void setLineThickness(int thickness)
        Deprecated. You will not be able to set physical thicknesses with this method. Please use setThickness(IXGraphicSize) method or setThickness(double, XGraphicsUnit) method instead.
        Sets the logical line thickness to the specified value.

        If the line thickness was physical, then it will become logical and change to the specified logical value.

        Parameters:
        thickness - line thickness

      • setPrimaryColor

        void setPrimaryColor(java.awt.Color primaryColor)
        Sets the primary color to the specified value.
        Parameters:
        primaryColor - primary color to be applied on an object

      • setSecondaryColor

        void setSecondaryColor(java.awt.Color secondaryColor)
        Sets the secondary color to the specified value.

        Note:The secondary color is applied only if the object is IXWire and line style is overidden that is different from default one IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.SOLID.
        Parameters:
        secondaryColor - secondary color to be applied on an object

      • getLineStyle

        IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE getLineStyle()
        Gets the specified line style. If line style is not overridden returns default line style IXGraphicsAttribute.LINE_STYLE.SOLID
        Returns:
        specified line style

      • getLineThickness

        @Deprecated
int getLineThickness()
        Deprecated. You will not ba able to get physical thicknesses with this method. Please use getThickness() method instead.
        Gets the specified logical line thickness.

        If line thickness is not overridden then returns default line thickness i.e. 1.

        If the line thickness is physical, then 0 will be returned from this method.

        Returns:
        specified line thickness

      • getPrimaryColor

        java.awt.Color getPrimaryColor()
        Gets the specified primary color.
        Returns:
        specified primary color. Otherwise null.

      • getSecondaryColor

        java.awt.Color getSecondaryColor()
        Gets the specified secondary color.
        Returns:
        specified secondary color. Otherwise null

      • setFillPattern

        void setFillPattern(FILL_PATTERN_STYLE fillPattern)
        Sets the fill pattern to the specified value.
        If the pattern set to FILL_PATTERN_STYLE.PATTERN_NONE, the fill foreground color and fill background colors are ignored.
        Parameters:
        fillPattern - fill pattern

      • setFillForegroundColor

        void setFillForegroundColor(java.awt.Color fillForegroundColor)
        Sets the foreground color to the specified value. If the NULL is passed, the system defaults to BLACK color.
        Parameters:
        fillForegroundColor - foreground fill color

      • setFillBackgroundColor

        void setFillBackgroundColor(java.awt.Color fillBackgroundColor)
        Sets the background color to the specified value. If the NULL is passed, the system defaults to BLACK color

        Currently, setting background color may not be helpful as only SOLID pattern is supported. This method is added for future extensions.

        Parameters:
        fillBackgroundColor - background fill color

      • getFillPattern

        FILL_PATTERN_STYLE getFillPattern()
        Gets the fill pattern.
        Returns:
        specified fill pattern

      • getFillForegroundColor

        java.awt.Color getFillForegroundColor()
        Gets the fill foreground color.
        Returns:
        specified fill foreground color

      • getFillBackgroundColor

        java.awt.Color getFillBackgroundColor()
        Gets the fill background color.
        Returns:
        specified fill background color

      • getThickness

        IXGraphicSize getThickness()
        Gets the specified line thickness. If line thickness is not overridden then returns default line thickness i.e. 1.
        Returns:
        specified line thickness

      • setThickness

        void setThickness(double value,
                  XGraphicsUnit unit)
        Sets the line thickness to the specified value.

        Both physical and logical values can be set.

        When you use this method to set a logcial thickness, please ensure that 'value' is a whole number. Otherwise the number will be rounded to the neareast integer.

        If you pass null as the 'unit', no changes to the graphic size will be made.

        Parameters:
        value - thickness size
        unit - thickness unit - LOGICAL, MM, CM or INCH

      • setThickness

        void setThickness(IXGraphicSize thickness)
        Sets the line thickness to the specified value.

        Both physical and logical values can be set.

        If you pass null as the 'thickness', no changes to the graphic size will be made to the object. Whatever the thickness was before, it will be now.

        Parameters:
        thickness - line thickness
